message:
fix(oracle): skip unobserved invariants in EProcessMonitor instead of treating them as passing

Previously, `observe_report` iterated all tracked e-processes and marked
any invariant absent from the report as "not violated" (passing). This is
incorrect: if the oracle didn't check an invariant (e.g., it was scoped
out of that run), the e-process monitor should leave the accumulator
unchanged rather than silently recording a passing observation.

Changed the loop to `if let Some(entry) = ...` so only invariants that
actually appear in the report get their e-value updated. This prevents
NaN-adjacent drift in the e-value accumulator for invariants that are
intermittently checked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
